objective: Implement dependency-free bounded core stream descriptors with explicit nominal-rate and channel-format values while keeping discovery, XML, runtime identity, recovery, and authority outside the crate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route core stream descriptor contracts while keeping source identifiers, discovery observations, runtime identity, and admission authority distinct.
Describe the exact local descriptor behavior without claiming XML, discovery, recovery, transport, runtime, or ecosystem interoperability.

Review whether the distinction between optional source correlation and runtime or admission identity exposes a reusable authority rule; avoid a repo-specific recipe when existing guidance is sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: XML parsing or serialization, XPath or query matching, metadata-tree mutation, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, sample encoding, channel conversion, or byte-size calculation.

Assigning protocol version, creation time, outlet UID, session ID, hostname, addresses, ports, or runtime recovery behavior; interpreting source_id as authorization, global identity, or Morphospace admission authority.
Reading clocks, measuring or enforcing sampling rate, scheduling, interpolation, buffering, queueing, threads, process spawning, FFI, native-library loading, provider selection, or language bindings.

Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, wire numeric discriminants, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, and production liblsl integration.

Morphospace adapters, Manifold admission or stream authority, platform lifecycle, Quest or Hostess behavior, device validation, and any wire, runtime, performance, recovery, or ecosystem compatibility claim.

acceptance.proof: Construction requires a nonempty bounded stream name, a bounded positive channel count, bounded optional content type and source identifier text, preserves accepted caller values without normalization, and rejects malformed limits or one-past inputs atomically with typed deterministic errors.

Nominal rate is explicitly irregular or a finite positive regular-Hz value whose bits are preserved, and the descriptor carries one of seven independently named channel data formats without assigning wire numeric codes or implementing encoding/conversion.

An optional source identifier remains opaque caller data for future correlation only; the local descriptor exposes no runtime UID, discovery result, recovery decision, route, permission, admission, or accepted Morphospace state.

Focused tests cover empty name, exact and one-past text/channel bounds, malformed limits, non-finite/non-positive regular rates, irregular rate, all named channel formats, unchanged floating-point bits and text, optional source correlation, and stable error payloads.
The crate remains one unpublished std-only package with no dependencies, features, unsafe code, clocks, XML, runtime effects, protocol implementation, or compatibility overclaim.

outputs: Dependency-free bounded core stream descriptor, nominal sample-rate, and channel-format Rust APIs with typed deterministic errors.
Positive and damaged unit tests plus a separate CORE-003 local contract overlay that preserves every historical STRM-000 result.
A source-identifier boundary that retains opaque correlation data without creating discovery, recovery, route, permission, or admission authority.
Deterministic CORE-003 validation entrypoint and exact public status updates that do not claim XML, protocol, discovery, runtime, wire, or ecosystem support.
commit_policy: Commit the accepted stream descriptor slice separately from XML, discovery, runtime identity, recovery, protocol, transport, adapters, or provider work.
